default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Deal Information

### Prospect

  - [Oleksandr Yaroshenko, Chief of Staff at Headway (fast-growing edtech with 5 products; \>170M downloads across 170+ countries; \~550 employees distributed across Spain, Ukraine, Cyprus, Poland). Leads Investor Relations; active on LinkedIn with \~4k followers.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=219.0)

### Call Context

  - [Intro call (referred by Carlos from rethoric). Headway exploring C‑suite-in-the-spotlight program via LinkedIn to strengthen employer brand, general brand awareness, and showcase leadership expertise. Internal comms team is under-resourced; Oleksandr currently self-writes posts.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=45.0)

### Seats

  - [Initial: 1 profile (Oleksandr) to pilot.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Target expansion: broad C‑suite and leadership cohort: CMO, CTO, COO, CPO, Head of People, and Oleksandr. Potentially CEO later.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)

### Budget

  - [Not explicitly stated. Prospect sought pricing tiers and discount structure. Acknowledged 3.5k/month per profile and discounting for volume; evaluating ROI vs hiring/employer branding goals. Interested in annual prepay discount.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1002.0)

### Current State

  - [In-house comms team manages CEO presence; Oleksandr writes his own content, posts somewhat regularly. Limited bandwidth for graphics/data visuals/reports. No external ghostwriting cadence or analytics benchmarking.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)

### Pain Points

  - [Under-resourced comms team can’t scale consistent executive content.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)
  - [Oleksandr lacks time for high-quality visuals/assets and deeper content production while running IR.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1920.0)
  - [Need to balance content for multiple goals (employer brand, investor interest, eventual B2B demand gen) without sounding self-promotional or AI-generated.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
  - [Unclear performance benchmarks and algorithm strategy; inconsistent results after posting pauses.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2340.0)

### Specific Requirements

  - [Consistency via a streamlined process (monthly interviews feeding 3–5 posts/week).](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)
  - [Strategy mix: growth (distribution), authority (industry POV), and selective conversion (talent applications via UTMs; investor intro calls).](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
  - [Performance tracking/benchmarks vs large dataset (percentiles by follower count) with monthly KPI summaries.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=583.0)
  - [Warm lead extraction from post engagers for future B2B conversion when relevant.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=855.0)
  - [Editorial quality: strong hooks, non-AI voice, persona-aligned narratives (Oleksandr’s lens: advice to earlier-stage founders that also signals to investors).](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=161.0)
  - [Visual guidance: prefers graphics/templates but open to evidence-based approach on what currently performs (contextual photos + text).](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1512.0)

### Tech Stack

  - [Not discussed in detail. Mentioned capability/interest in UTM tracking for recruiting and potential CRM/sales team handoff for warm outreach.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)

### Objections

  - [Visual production: Oleksandr expected vendor-created graphics/templates aligned to brand. Edu clarified graphics aren’t core because they underperform vs contextual photos/text; exception for one-off announcements. Objection partially addressed by data/examples and process notes.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1512.0)
  - [ROI for non-demand-gen use case: Concern over cost vs visuals he could DIY. Addressed by positioning value in strategy, consistency, hooks, benchmarking, and multi-profile program impact; still to validate via pilot outcomes.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1920.0)

### Other Solutions Under Evaluation

  - [None named. Oleksandr referenced admired profiles (e.g., Andrew Young; Aleona) as quality bar, not vendors.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1789.0)

### Timeline

  - [Pilot now with 1 profile to “easier way into the organization,” then expand to more accounts/leaders.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Headway’s B2B product MVP mid-March; launch likely April. That team could become a client for demand gen focus soon after.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1086.0)

### Decision Maker

  - [Not explicitly defined. Oleksandr appears primary sponsor for pilot; broader rollout may involve CMO/leadership and potentially CEO.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)

### Procurement Process

  - [Not detailed. Oleksandr asked about minimum term and prepay discounts, implying internal approval contingent on pricing/ROI and possibly annual prepay consideration.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2232.0)

### Next steps

  - [Eduardo to send formal proposal with:](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2223.0)
      - [Tiered pricing (3.5k per profile; 3k at 2 seats; 2.8k at 3+)](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1441.0)
      - [Posting cadence options (3x/week base; up to daily with higher tier)](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=431.0)
      - [Interview cadence (60–90 mins/month)](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=431.0)
      - [Monthly KPI summary methodology](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=583.0)
      - [Annual prepay option with 20% discount](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2232.0)
  - [Oleksandr to review internally, align on pilot profile (self) and timing relative to B2B launch.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2223.0)
  - [If approved: schedule first monthly interview; define content strategy pillars (growth/authority/conversion split), target audiences (talent, investors, future B2B ICP), and photo asset plan.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2223.0)

## Q\&A

### Questions We Asked

#### Who on the C‑suite would participate?

  - [Oleksandr confirmed initial seat for himself; future expansion to CMO, CTO, COO, CPO, Head of People; CEO currently led by comms.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=154.0)

#### Company profile and scale (team size, funding, geography)?

  - [Headway scaled bootstrapped until 2025; now \~550 people across ES/UA/CY/PL; 170M+ downloads in 170+ countries; 5 edtech products.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=219.0)

#### Goals for content (demand gen vs brand)?

  - [Primary: employer branding and general brand awareness; authority building. Secondary conversions: recruiting via UTMs and investor interest; later B2B demand gen.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)

#### Visual/content collaboration and what performs on LinkedIn?

  - [Edu outlined current best performers: contextual photos + strong text or text-only; carousels/infographics now underperform. Rethoric doesn’t routinely create graphics; will guide on contextual images and can produce occasional announcement graphics.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1512.0)

#### Pricing, tiers, discounts?

  - [Edu shared: 3.5k/mo/profile (3x/week, 1 monthly interview); 5k/10k tiers for added complexity (newsletters, 7x/week); volume discounts (2 seats = 3k each; 3+ = 2.8k each); annual prepay 20% discount.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1441.0)

#### Expected outcomes/benchmarks?

  - [Edu: can likely double profile visits in first 2 months; grow Oleksandr from \~4k to \>10k followers in \~12 months with consistency; long-term large-scale growth (e.g., 150k) requires 3+ years at 5x/week and tight content-market fit.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2361.0)

### Questions They Asked

#### Tiers/pricing and fit given limited direct conversion today?

  - [Edu positioned value in awareness/employer brand + investor interest; shared tiered pricing and volume discounts; suggested warm outreach from engager extraction for future B2B.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1002.0)

#### Contract term and prepayment discounts?

  - [Annual prepay available with 20% discount; month-to-month otherwise. Proposal will include both options.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=2232.0)

#### Content plan approval, ability to make changes, and who handles visuals?

  - [Collaborative process: monthly interview feeds drafts; client reviews/edits in platform; Rethoric specifies needed contextual images in notes; client supplies photos. Occasional one-off graphics for announcements possible. Strategy adjusts to performance.](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1512.0)

#### Examples of B2C LinkedIn strategy and hiring-focused executive branding?

  - [Edu shared sample B2C profile using frequent viral CTAs; and Checkr CTO example focused on engineering thought leadership to drive hiring, with light conversion cadence (e.g., hiring posts biweekly).](https://fathom.video/share/XzkqketH-DRpsJMFaU7Bqy_7s8_LMGPv?tab=summary&timestamp=1157.0)

meeting_title: Rethoric intro call: Edu and Oleksandr Yaroshenko
title: Rethoric intro call: Edu and Oleksandr Yaroshenko

transcript.text: Hi, hi, hi.
How are you?
How are you?
I'm good, I'm good.
The sun is shining, the weather is nice in Madrid.
How are you?
I'm good, I'm good.
That always happens to me.
I say, how are you and the other person at the same time.
How are you?
Are you in Ukraine or where are you calling from?
No, I'm in Madrid.
We have an office in Madrid as well.
Oh, nice, nice.
Do you speak Spanish?
Un poquito.
Muy bien, muy bien.
Well, great meeting you, Alexander.
I'm Edo, I'm the founder of Rhetoric.
I think that you were in contact with Carlos from my team, right?
And I guess that the idea here is to put more content out there for the CEO of Headway or what's the plan?
So basically, basically, we have an idea that...
So basically C-Suite in the Spotlight via LinkedIn content and having some sort of title writers, ghostwriters for our C-Suite is a good idea in many terms.
It's mainly not for demand generation, but rather for correct employer brand as well as just general awareness of our company as well as expertise in the field of each of the members is a good thing.

And we have already like our own internal communications team, yet as a fast growing company, that's like a typical pain that you are under-resourced and our communications team is no acceptance to that.
So I am, for example, writing my own content as well as like while CO is...

Being led by our communications team mostly, so from this perspective, VC demands value in just putting the C-suite and just leadership of the team to the LinkedIn media in general, with varying type of content.
And so basically to have this new media outlet that we run.
Got it, got it.
Okay, so you're interested in doing this for the C-suite, like who specifically on the C-suite?
So it will be me, chief of staff, while I'm running mostly investor relations.
And the way I positioned my content is that I basically target entrepreneurs and give advice to probably an earlier stage entrepreneurs.
However, as
The lens of the advice is such that if investors read, they would seem the thought process quite reasonable and that this company might be interesting for them.
So this is like my personal lens that I developed for myself and that I tried to use.
But for the rest, it would be CMO, CTO, COO, as well as CPO, if I didn't mention.
So basically, everyone as well as head of people.
So we don't have like a chief HR officer, but we have head of people, which is on the same level.
Okay.
Okay.
Got it.
Can you tell me a little bit more about the company?
Like how many people is in the company?
I think it's a bootstrap business or is it VC backed or how is it?
So there is like no quick answer to that.
But overall, we were bootstrapped until 2025 and the company has grown from...
zero to 550 people.
It was rooted in Ukraine, but right now it's a global company with more than 170 million downloads over 170 countries.
We have five different products in the educational technology area, and our mission is to help people grow via redefining the life of learning.
So the company, yes, as I mentioned, it's 550 people right now that are distributed across Spain, Ukraine, Cyprus, and Poland.
Got it.
I see.
Great, great.
Perfect.
Okay.
So you said you're looking to do this for like four to five different profiles.
Is that right?
I'm more of a safe zone guy, and also I don't have enough time to develop graphics or create some sort of reports that you have to engage with the post to get access to, and so on.

So there is definitely a big upside potential in that, and also I'm kind of disciplined about posting regularly, so as I have seen in your presentation in advance, that you have this cooperative mode, it's not like 100% on you, but rather a cooperation of efforts between two people, and I would be a good example of that because I'm already doing that, so I'm interested in that, and it could be just an easier way into the organization to talk about more accounts to lead.
Yeah, yeah, totally.
Yeah, so the strategy is everything.
Like, there's two really important parts.
One of them is posting consistently.
Which is the hardest part, to be honest.
It's very difficult if you try to come up with a new idea, block some time to write the content, all that.
So we make all that very easy by doing a monthly interview.
And it's a one-hour call.
And from that call, we can extract around 12 different topics.
So we can post three times per week.
If you want to post more often, we can do that by posting five times a week.
But we would need like 90-minute sessions, one hour and a half every month.
So that tackles the main problem, which is consistency.
From there, we need to think about strategy.
And for strategy, it depends on what are your goals.
So you already told me that it's not demand generation or anything like that.
It's more like brand awareness, employee advocacy, and that type of stuff.
So...
So...
There's three types of content.
Growth content, authority, content, and conversion.
So in this case, conversion is the least relevant.
The first two are the most important ones.
It actually could be a conversion, but in each way.
So basically, in my specific case, it could be two things that we could be optimizing for.
One could be getting recommendations of people or getting people who apply via, for example, special UTM mark to certain job openings via my posts.
But another one could be, for example, getting investors interested in us and getting, like, some sort of a kindly invite to get an intro call with me.
But also for me to sort who is getting in, who is not, who is getting some sort of an auto-reply that you are just...
Not our interest zone, but also cert, probably just a cert that came up to my mind would be, yeah, I mean, it just came and slipped.
Okay, whatever.
If I recall, I will have them.
Yeah, yeah, Because there are some options that we can track the performance of the post as well and how specific we want the audience.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah.
So, you know, growth content is usually to get more distribution, more impressions, more followers.
Depending on the amount of followers, it usually correlates the amount of impressions that you're going to have.
We, every month, we send a monthly report that explains, wait, it's not a monthly report, it's a monthly KPI summary because those are the most important things, comparing to 50,000 accounts.
So, that way, we can really understand.
let's see.
How the account is performing because the LinkedIn algorithm is private, so we really don't know exactly what is working, but we have some signs based on what other accounts are doing.

So our approach is comparing the performance with 50,000 different accounts, and then we can see, based on the number of followers that you have, if the posts are getting the median in terms of impressions in the 25th percentile or in the 90th percentile or in the 1%.
And that's how we can really understand, like, if we're going into the right direction or not in terms of impressions.
So that's for the growth content.
So that's, think about that as a top of funnel because you need to have a lot of impressions, lot of brand awareness, distribution.
From there,
You need to start posting content that is authority, which is speaking about the industry that the company is in without speaking about the company itself, right?
So just lessons, mistakes, frameworks, just to give value to other people without asking anything in return.
That's the most important thing.
Just sharing, sharing, sharing.
When you do that, that builds trust.
If you build trust, people are going to be interested.
And this is very important for the investors.
If you just share, share, share, share in a strategic way by saying, we've been growing and things, but without promoting the company, it makes them very curious.
Like, this guy is not crying too hard, but it looks like he's doing very well.
So I want to know them, you know?
And in the end, you have conversion, which usually...
Conversion is for the end customer, which is using case studies or using things like that.
But in your case, I don't think it makes a lot of sense because it's more like a B2C company, right?

So basically we have mostly B2C audience right now, but eventually one of our sales modes, businesses that we are, well, business lines that we are right now working on, that is being developed by an in-house team, they will be launching, for example, B2B, as well as we have like a B2B sort of a packaging for our B2C solution.
So it's like an employee benefits or there is like partnerships with Revolut across several countries.
So basically every Revolut Ultra card holder has access to one of our products called Headway.
So, and some similar things are happening across the board.
So we have like more than 400 different...
Like brands with whom we have B2B relations, yet most of our business is still B2C.
Got it, got it.
Okay.
Well, in that case, that would make a lot of sense.
Like the thing is that in B2B usually, or like if you try to find like an ROI from organic content, it works when the average contract value is very high.
And what I mean very high, more than $50,000.
That way you can get one or two customers and just, you know, pay for the entire year of the service.
And you get for free like all the run awareness and everything else, you know.
So that's when it makes sense.
When it doesn't make a lot of sense is when you're selling like a 10-month subscription or something like that.

Where you would have to have like thousands of people, which in that case is more like growth content, trying to go viral as much as possible and doing some tactics to extract leads, which is also something that we can do.
We can get everyone that engages on each post.
We can extract their LinkedIn profile.
Once that we have their profile, we enrich them by having their email, their phone number, all the details about themselves and their company.
And by having all of that data, we can then filter them to only keep the ones that are on your ICP.
And when we have that list, we push that to your sales team so they can reach out to them.
And that's not called outreach.
That's warm outreach because these are people that already know the company and already know the people in the company because they have...
been engaging with their content, but the sales team is reaching out to them.
So their side, it's like, oh, what a coincidence.
I've been engaging with the content of this guy.
It's only the sales guy reached out to me.
And if they're the ICP, they're going to be naturally interested.
So that's a strategy that works really, really well.
If you want to do conversion at some point.

But yeah, if you try to think about this as, I mean, I know that you said initially it would be one profile, but if you think about many different profiles, the best approach is doing like on a vertical basis.
So each profile based on the expertise that they have, focusing a specific vertical, a specific, because they, each one of them have a specific experience and trajectory.
And that's really important actually, because the algorithm is built in a way that you need to be speaking about the things that you actually know and LinkedIn knows.
Based on where you have been working on.
So, for example, if you've been working on startups for the last 10 years and suddenly you start talking about cooking, it's not going to work.
Like the algorithm is not going to distribute your content because he knows that you're not an experienced person in that topic.
Like, you know, that is why it's really important to pick the right people to define the right narrative when doing these employee advocacy programs, which are, yeah.
But anyways, do you have any specific questions that I can answer to you?
Yeah, probably the one thing, because it's going to be very obvious.
So, like the tiers and pricing of your services and how you can see it working with, for example, our not so much optimized conversions, at least at this point in time.

So we could definitely calculate, for example, ROI on closed vacancies, but again, the average price for that is definitely not like 50K for us internally, least from what we were estimating in terms of having calculated the average close time and the price of a recruiter, et cetera, et cetera.

While B2B contracts are usually, like, they defer, if we can unlock the audience and basically open up, like, accounts who will buy, like, 100 different, 100 of subscriptions per account, so to say, then it might make sense because, like, on average, our subscription for B2B would be maybe, I don't know, so So
$35, I think, for a year or something like that.
So we can calculate some sort of unit economy there.

But again, if you're talking about primarily driving audience and awareness as a first goal, but then eventually probably we will also bring the team who is developing from grown-up B2B products and that will be released quite likely by mid-March, I think, or or maybe closer to April.
So probably the MVP will be in mid-March for public testing, but the real launch may be in April.
So that could be also a prospective client because for them demand generation is kind of crucial.
And also they will be focusing on English-speaking markets, primarily UK, US.
So most of our business is B2C in the US.
So more than 50% of our revenue comes from the US.
So, yeah, there is definitely a room to grow in terms of cooperation, but just to get a sense of how it works and to test the pilots, you know, what our priorities are.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, totally.
Let me share you a profile.
So here's a profile, much review, you know, this guy.
I just sent it here on the messages.
Just a second.
This is a very good example of someone that is selling a B2C product on LinkedIn, and the strategy is just putting viral content that has the highest probabilities on going viral.
And at the end of each post, if you notice, at the end of each post...

As often as possible for the sake of getting as many people on the pipeline to convert, but then having the other profiles, maybe the CEO or the CMO or whoever, doing more like a thought leadership for brand advocacy, for employee engagement, or the chief of, not the chief of, head of people, you said?
Yeah.
Do more related to how amazing is working at Headway, without saying that directly, you know, but making people excited.
And that's how you create a strategy that connects with all of the goals that you're looking for.
So, I can give you an example.
I'm not sure if you know Checkr, the background checks company.
No, no, I don't.
It's a huge company.
They're making one build.
So here's a profile of the CTO.

And the example with this guy is that he's positioning himself as a thought leader for the engineering space because the goal is to make him a very visual person in the technical space to get more engineers.
Into the company.
So this is an example of a strategy just for that, to hire engineers.
And if you look at it, it's not like he's speaking constantly about, yeah, we're hiring engineers.
But he's getting the eyeballs of engineers.
You know?
So the conversion in this case is maybe once every two weeks.
He puts like, we are hiring engineers, blah, blah, blah.
don't yeah, yeah.
Yeah, well, in terms of pricing, our plans start at 3.5K per month, per profile, for posting three times a week, one monthly interview.
And from there, we have a 5K plan and a 10K plan, depending on the complexity, if you want a newsletter, if you want to post seven times a week.
And we do discounts based on the amount of people when we are managing more than one.
So, yeah, what would be the potential range of discounts from one to five?
Okay, so it's a tiered pricing.
So for one is 3.5K, for two, it goes down to 3K per profile, for three and beyond, it goes to 2.8K per profile.
And that's like the minimum that we can manage on a per-profile basis.
I see.
Okay, I got it.
So does the content plan somehow get agreed and then there could be changes to the content, also you develop the visuals, how the specifics works?
Yes, but it depends on what is performing well.
So for example, two years ago, carousels, infographics, things like that were performing very well.
Not anymore.
So right now we are not doing those type of things because they are not performing well.
What is performing well is pictures, like real pictures.
If you're saying that you're speaking, I saw your latest post that you were going to speak or use.
Something about a webinar or something like that.
So that makes sense.
Like, there's a picture where you appear and it adds visual context to the information of the post.
Those are the ones that perform well.
And the thing is that we cannot create graphics.
What performs well is pictures.
So what we do is every time that we send the batch of content for review, we have a notes section in the platform that we use.
And in the notes section, we include, please include a picture about speaking with a team or a picture doing X, Y, and Z.
So you just need to put the picture in there or take the picture and just add it there because that's what is working right now.
Thank you.
Thank you.
So basically, LinkedIn has become an Instagram.
It's pictures of faces and videos.

Yes, but there's a difference because there's a lot of people that are starting to use LinkedIn as kind of like Instagram, which is mainly content creators, which are uploading pictures just for the sake of adding a picture to the post.
And then them looking at the horizon or them like just working at the computer and someone taking a picture of them working.
Those don't usually perform well.
Like what it really performs well is a picture that adds visual context to the information of the post.
